payload.source_text: High Level Description
The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.
High Level Description
The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL ZachmanCell elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Claim task completion function enabling children to record work done
payload.justification: Signal describes the core business capability for children to claim they have completed earning tasks.

payload.description: Task specification function enabling parents to define work and rewards
payload.justification: Signal identifies the business capability for parents to specify tasks with associated monetary values.

payload.description: Task viewing function for children to see available and completed work
payload.justification: Signal describes business capability enabling children to view both available tasks and their own completions.

payload.description: Weekly earnings aggregation process summarizing child task completion rewards
payload.justification: Signal defines a business process that aggregates and reports earnings at the end of each weekly period.

payload.description: Payment reconciliation process enabling parent review of child earnings
payload.justification: Signal identifies a business process where parents view earnings reports for payment reconciliation purposes.

payload.description: Task completion events must be associated with the completing child
payload.justification: Signal explicitly states a conceptual relationship rule governing how completions are linked to children.

payload.description: All tasks must return to available state at weekly reset
payload.justification: Signal defines a business rule governing task state transitions during the weekly cycle reset.

payload.description: Child as a business entity participating in the pocket money system
payload.justification: Multiple signals clearly establish Child as a distinct business entity that claims tasks, completes work, views tasks, and accumulates earnings.

payload.description: Parent as a business entity managing the pocket money system
payload.justification: Signals identify Parent as a business entity that specifies tasks and views reports for reconciliation purposes.

payload.description: Task as a work unit entity with monetary reward value
payload.justification: Task is clearly a core business entity that is specified, claimed, completed, viewed, and reset across multiple signals.

payload.description: Task completion event as a business fact entity
payload.justification: Completion events are identified as entities that must be associated with children and retained as historical records.

payload.description: Monetary reward value associated with tasks
payload.justification: Signal explicitly identifies reward values as data attributes of task entities.

payload.description: Weekly earnings aggregate calculated per child
payload.justification: Earnings are data attributes aggregated from task completions for each child within the weekly period.

payload.description: Task completion status indicating available or completed state
payload.justification: Signals describe tasks having states (available vs completed) that change through the weekly cycle.

payload.description: Association between task completion and completing child
payload.justification: Signal explicitly defines a required conceptual relationship linking completion events to child entities.

payload.description: Ownership relationship between parent and specified tasks
payload.justification: Parents specifying tasks implies a business relationship between parent entity and task entity.

payload.description: Weekly operational cycle governing task availability and earnings calculation
payload.justification: Multiple signals establish a weekly cycle as the fundamental temporal structure for task claiming, earnings aggregation, and reset.

payload.description: Task completion event occurring when child claims work done
payload.justification: Signals describe task completion as a business event that occurs during the weekly cycle and must be associated with the child.

payload.description: Weekly period end event triggering earnings aggregation
payload.justification: Signal identifies period end as a business event that triggers the earnings reporting process.

payload.description: Weekly reset trigger returning all tasks to available state
payload.justification: Signal defines a weekly reset as a timing trigger that causes tasks to transition to available state.

payload.description: Child role performing task completion and earnings accumulation
payload.justification: Signals identify Child as a business role that claims tasks, views work, and receives earnings.

payload.description: Parent role managing task specification and payment reconciliation
payload.justification: Signals identify Parent as a business role responsible for specifying tasks and reviewing earnings for payment.

payload.description: Enable children to earn pocket money through task completion
payload.justification: Core business concept establishes the fundamental goal of the system to enable earning through task completion.

payload.description: Provide parents with visibility for payment reconciliation
payload.justification: Signal identifies payment reconciliation as a business objective supported by parent viewing of earnings reports.

payload.description: Task completion history must be retained beyond current period
payload.justification: Signal explicitly mandates retention and accessibility of historical completion data as a business constraint.

payload.description: Task availability operates on a recurring weekly cycle basis
payload.justification: Weekly reset cycle represents a business principle governing how task availability is structured temporally.

payload.description: Aggregate child earnings by weekly period boundary
payload.justification: Temporal aggregation logic for earnings calculation is a logical function triggered at weekly boundaries

payload.description: Generate parent reporting view of aggregated child task and earnings data
payload.justification: Logical reporting interface is a function that transforms and presents aggregated data to parents

payload.description: Reset task availability state at weekly interval boundaries
payload.justification: Cyclical state reset logic is a process that executes at defined temporal intervals to update task status

payload.description: Task completion establishes assignment relationship between Task and Child
payload.justification: Signals describe logical relationship mechanism that associates task to child upon completion event, clearly a What/Relationship at Designer level

payload.description: Task availability status attribute subject to cyclical reset
payload.justification: State reset logic implies a logical attribute (availability status) of Task entity that changes state

payload.description: Child earnings aggregate derived from task completion data
payload.justification: Aggregated child earnings represents a logical data entity computed from task completion relationships

payload.description: Weekly period boundary triggers earnings calculation aggregation
payload.justification: Temporal aggregation keyed to weekly boundary is explicitly a trigger event for calculation logic

payload.description: Weekly interval cycle governing task availability reset
payload.justification: Weekly interval boundaries defining cyclical state reset clearly represent a temporal cycle at Designer level

payload.description: Task completion event establishing child-task association
payload.justification: Task completion is the logical event that triggers the relationship establishment mechanism

payload.description: Parent role authorized to view aggregated child earnings and task completion data
payload.justification: Reporting interface providing data 'to parents' implies a logical Parent role with viewing authorization

payload.description: Temporal task data must persist beyond current cycle boundary
payload.justification: Data retention requirement for task data persistence is a design constraint governing temporal data lifecycle

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL CellContentItem elements present in the ledger.

payload.name: Task Stewardship
payload.description: Business responsibility for defining, maintaining, and governing the work units offered within the pocket money system, including their monetary value, availability cycles, and reset obligations across weekly periods.

payload.name: Completion Accountability
payload.description: Business responsibility for recording and preserving the factual claims of work performed by participants, establishing persistent associations between completion events and the responsible child, and ensuring historical integrity beyond current operating cycles.

payload.name: Earnings Settlement
payload.description: Business responsibility for periodic aggregation of earned value attributable to each child participant, enabling parental oversight and reconciliation of payment obligations arising from completed work within defined operating cycles.

payload.name: Task-Child Assignment Model
payload.description: Logical structure and behavior governing the association between tasks and children through completion events, including the persistence requirements and lifecycle state of task availability attributes.

payload.name: Earnings Derivation Logic
payload.description: Logical model for deriving child earnings aggregates from task completion data, including the temporal period boundaries that trigger aggregation and the persistence constraints governing historical earnings data.

payload.name: Parent Visibility Model
payload.description: Logical design of parental access to aggregated child performance data, defining the role-based view structure and the scope of authorized information disclosure across child tasks and earnings.

payload.name: Cyclical State Reset Logic
payload.description: Logical behavior governing the periodic reset of task availability state, driven by weekly cycle boundaries and enforcing temporal consistency across task assignment periods.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Domain elements present in the ledger.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a Task as a work unit entity with an associated monetary reward value.
payload.rationale: Defines the core structural entity and its essential monetary attribute that the business must maintain within the task stewardship domain.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a completion status for each task indicating whether the task is available or completed.
payload.rationale: Defines the state attribute required to govern task lifecycle within the weekly operational cycle.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain an ownership relationship between each parent and their specified tasks.
payload.rationale: Establishes the structural relationship that governs task accountability and parental authority over task specification.

payload.statement: The business shall enforce a weekly operational cycle governing task availability.
payload.rationale: Establishes the temporal governance rule constraining task availability to operate on a recurring weekly basis, derived from the temporal cycle and the principle that underpins it.

payload.statement: The business shall return all tasks to available state at each weekly reset.
payload.rationale: Expresses the business responsibility to execute the state transition triggered by the weekly reset cycle, implementing the task availability governance rule.

payload.statement: The system shall make task completion claimable by children.
payload.rationale: System-subject affordance naming the black-box capability provided at the boundary to enable child claiming actions. Complementary to the actor-action statement above.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of each task completion event.
payload.rationale: Business-subject responsibility to preserve task completion as a business fact entity (What). Expresses the behind-boundary obligation to record completion events.

payload.statement: The business shall associate each task completion event with the completing child.
payload.rationale: Business constraint enforcing the structural relationship (What) and the business rule (How) that completion events must carry child attribution. This is a governance obligation on data integrity.

payload.statement: The business shall retain task completion history beyond the current operating period.
payload.rationale: Why-column constraint on historical data retention expressing the business governance rule for persistence beyond current cycles.

payload.statement: The business shall enable children to earn pocket money through task completion.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility to support the earning capability that fulfills the system's motivating goal (Why). This is the behind-boundary enablement of the earn-through-work model.

payload.statement: The business shall recognise a child as a participating business entity within the pocket money system.
payload.rationale: Establishes the child as a structural entity with the associated role of task completion and earnings accumulation at the business level.

payload.statement: The business shall recognise a parent as a participating business entity within the pocket money system.
payload.rationale: Establishes the parent as a structural entity with the associated role of task specification and payment reconciliation at the business level.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a weekly earnings aggregate for each child.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility for maintaining the weekly earnings aggregate artefact, derived from the weekly earnings aggregation process that summarizes child task completion rewards.

payload.statement: The system shall make a child's earnings visible to the parent for payment reconciliation.
payload.rationale: Complementary system affordance enabling the parent's review capability, providing visibility for payment reconciliation at the system boundary.

payload.statement: The business shall enforce a weekly operational cycle governing task availability and earnings calculation.
payload.rationale: Business governance rule establishing the temporal constraint on business operations, derived from the weekly cycle CCI.

payload.statement: The business shall perform earnings aggregation at the end of each weekly period.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility for executing earnings aggregation triggered by the weekly period end event.

payload.statement: A parent can define tasks, specifying the work and associated rewards.
payload.rationale: CCI-ROW2-C-How-002 describes a task specification function (How-column) enabling parents to define work and rewards. At Row 2, this is an ACTOR subject statement — the parent is an external party interacting WITH the system (boundary-crossing action). The verb 'define' is the parent's real action. The statement names what the parent does through the system boundary, not how the system realizes it.

payload.statement: A child can view available and completed tasks.
payload.rationale: CCI-ROW2-C-How-003 describes a task viewing function (How-column) for children to see work. At Row 2, this is an ACTOR subject statement — the child is an external party interacting WITH the system (boundary-crossing). The verb 'view' is the child's real action. The object 'available and completed tasks' captures both states mentioned in the source CCI.

payload.statement: The business shall recognise task completion events when a child claims work done.
payload.rationale: CCI-ROW2-C-When-002 describes a task completion event (When-column) occurring when a child claims work done. At Row 2, this is a BUSINESS subject responsibility — the business must recognise and respond to this event trigger behind the boundary. The statement captures the business's obligation to handle the completion event without specifying the technical mechanism (Row 3 HOW).

payload.statement: The logical model shall define Task as an entity carrying a monetary reward attribute and an availability status attribute subject to cyclical reset.
payload.rationale: Refines R001 by specifying Task as a logical entity with the reward attribute and incorporating the availability status attribute identified in the domain model.
payload.fit_criteria: Entity definition includes both reward and availability status attributes

payload.statement: The logical model shall define the Task availability status attribute to represent whether the task is available or completed within the current cycle.
payload.rationale: Refines R002 by specifying the availability status as a defined attribute within the logical data model.
payload.fit_criteria: Attribute definition supports at least two states: available and completed

payload.statement: The logical model shall define an ownership relationship from Parent to Task entities.
payload.rationale: Refines R003 by expressing the ownership as a logical relationship structure between parent and task entities.
payload.fit_criteria: Relationship model links each Task instance to exactly one Parent instance

payload.statement: The logical model shall define a weekly interval cycle governing task availability lifecycle.
payload.rationale: Refines R004 by specifying the weekly cycle as a logical temporal constraint in the model.
payload.fit_criteria: Cycle definition specifies weekly period boundaries

payload.statement: The logical model shall define a reset process that transitions all task availability states to available at weekly interval boundaries.
payload.rationale: Refines R005 by specifying the reset as a logical process tied to weekly boundaries.
payload.fit_criteria: Process specification resets availability attribute for all tasks at cycle boundary

payload.statement: The logical model shall define task completion as a claimable function invokable by child entities.
payload.rationale: Refines R006 by expressing task completion as a logical function associated with child role.
payload.fit_criteria: Function definition permits child entity to invoke task completion

payload.statement: The logical model shall define a task completion event entity persisting task completion occurrences.
payload.rationale: Refines R007 by specifying task completion as a discrete event entity in the logical model.
payload.fit_criteria: Event entity captures each task completion occurrence

payload.statement: The logical model shall define the task completion event to establish an association relationship between Task and Child entities.
payload.rationale: Refines R008 by specifying the completion event as the mechanism establishing task-child relationship.
payload.fit_criteria: Event structure links Task instance to Child instance

payload.statement: The logical model shall constrain task completion data to persist beyond the current weekly cycle boundary.
payload.rationale: Refines R009 by expressing retention as a persistence constraint spanning multiple cycles.
payload.fit_criteria: Data persistence constraint permits retrieval beyond current cycle

payload.statement: The logical model shall derive child earnings by aggregating monetary reward values from task completion events associated with each child.
payload.rationale: Refines R010 by specifying earnings derivation as an aggregation function over completion events.
payload.fit_criteria: Derivation function sums reward values from child-associated completions

payload.statement: The logical model shall define Child as an entity participating in task assignment relationships and earnings derivation.
payload.rationale: Refines R011 by specifying Child as a logical entity within the task and earnings domains.
payload.fit_criteria: Entity definition permits relationship to task completion and earnings

payload.statement: The logical model shall define Parent as an entity owning tasks and authorized to view aggregated child data.
payload.rationale: Refines R012 by specifying Parent as a logical entity with ownership and visibility roles.
payload.fit_criteria: Entity definition supports task ownership and view authorization relationships

payload.statement: The logical model shall define child earnings aggregate as a derived entity calculated over weekly period boundaries.
payload.rationale: Refines R013 by specifying weekly earnings as a derived logical entity in the model.
payload.fit_criteria: Derived entity represents aggregate earnings per child per weekly period

payload.statement: The logical model shall define a parent reporting view function generating aggregated child task and earnings data.
payload.rationale: Refines R014 by specifying visibility as a reporting view function accessible by parent role.
payload.fit_criteria: View function produces aggregated task completion and earnings data accessible to parent

payload.statement: The logical model shall define weekly interval cycle boundaries triggering both task availability reset and earnings aggregation.
payload.rationale: Refines R015 by specifying the weekly cycle as a unified temporal constraint governing both processes.
payload.fit_criteria: Cycle boundary definition triggers both reset and aggregation functions

payload.statement: The logical model shall define an earnings calculation function triggered by weekly period boundary events to aggregate child earnings.
payload.rationale: Refines R016 by specifying aggregation as a function triggered at weekly boundaries.
payload.fit_criteria: Function executes at end of weekly period and produces earnings aggregate

payload.statement: The logical model shall define task creation as a function invokable by parent entities, accepting work description and reward value attributes.
payload.rationale: Refines R017 by expressing task definition as a logical function with parent role authorization.
payload.fit_criteria: Function accepts work and reward parameters and creates task entity associated with parent

payload.statement: The logical model shall define a child view function providing access to task availability status and completion history.
payload.rationale: Refines R018 by specifying task viewing as a logical function accessible to child entities.
payload.fit_criteria: View function returns task list with availability and completion status for child entity

payload.statement: The logical model shall define task completion recognition as an event generated when a child entity claims work completion.
payload.rationale: Refines R019 by specifying completion recognition as event generation triggered by child claim.
payload.fit_criteria: Child claim action generates task completion event establishing child-task association

payload.statement: The system shall establish a logical association between a task instance and a child instance upon task completion.
payload.rationale: Task completion event triggers the creation of the assignment relationship; both the relationship structure (What) and the triggering event (When) express the same functional obligation.

payload.statement: The system shall maintain an availability state attribute for each task instance.
payload.rationale: The task entity must structurally possess an availability status attribute that is subject to lifecycle changes.

payload.statement: The system shall persist task-child association data across cycle boundaries.
payload.rationale: Temporal constraint governing data retention policy; the system is constrained to maintain historical assignment records beyond the current operational cycle.

payload.statement: The system shall maintain a logical association between each child entity and an earnings aggregate derived from task completion data.
payload.rationale: Defines the logical entity structure whereby child earnings aggregates are composed from task completion data; represents a structural relationship between child entity, earnings aggregate, and underlying task data.

payload.statement: The system shall aggregate child earnings by weekly period boundary.
payload.rationale: Specifies the logical aggregation function applied to child earnings, using weekly period as the temporal grouping boundary.

payload.statement: The system shall transition to earnings calculation aggregation state when a weekly period boundary is reached.
payload.rationale: Defines the logical state transition triggered by the temporal event (weekly period boundary) that initiates earnings aggregation processing.

payload.statement: The system shall enforce that temporal task data remains accessible beyond the current cycle boundary.
payload.rationale: Establishes a logical persistence constraint ensuring task data integrity across temporal boundaries, necessary for accurate historical earnings derivation.
payload.fit_criteria: Task data for any completed task shall be retrievable after the cycle in which it was completed has ended.

payload.statement: The system shall provide a logical view that aggregates child task completion and earnings data for access by the parent role.
payload.rationale: Derives from the How-column reporting view function and the Who-column authorization scope; expresses the logical capability to surface aggregated child data within the parent visibility boundary.

payload.statement: The system shall enforce that access to aggregated child task and earnings data is restricted to the parent role.
payload.rationale: Derives from the Who-column role authorization boundary; expresses the logical access control constraint that limits visibility of child performance data to the parent role only.
payload.fit_criteria: No user outside the parent role may retrieve or view aggregated child task or earnings data.

payload.statement: The system shall maintain a task availability status attribute that governs whether a task may be claimed in the current cycle.
payload.rationale: Defines the logical state attribute that controls task availability and is subject to cyclical reset operations.

payload.statement: The system shall transition task availability states to their initial values at weekly interval boundaries.
payload.rationale: Specifies the logical state-transition behavior that resets task availability at the conclusion of each weekly cycle, ensuring temporal consistency across assignment periods.
payload.fit_criteria: All task availability states return to their defined initial values upon weekly boundary crossing.

payload.description: Section header indicating the conceptual overview of the system follows.
Core business concept establishing the system enables children to claim completion of pocket money earning tasks during a weekly cycle.
Business capability defining that parents specify tasks with associated monetary reward values.
Conceptual relationship rule requiring task completion events to be associated with the completing child entity.
Business capability requiring children view both available and their own completed tasks within the current week.
Business capability defining weekly aggregation reporting of earnings for each child at period end.
Business capability enabling parents to view weekly earnings and completion reports per child for payment reconciliation.
Business rule defining weekly reset cycle where all tasks return to available state.
Business requirement mandating retention and accessibility of task completion history beyond current period.
Logical relationship mechanism establishing task-to-child association upon completion.
Temporal aggregation logic trigger keyed to weekly period boundary for earnings calculation.
Logical reporting interface providing aggregated child earnings and task completion data to parents.
Cyclical state reset logic that resets task availability status at weekly interval boundaries.
Data retention requirement for temporal task data persistence beyond current cycle.
Logical relationship mechanism establishing task-to-child association upon completion.
Temporal aggregation logic trigger keyed to weekly period boundary for earnings calculation.
Logical reporting interface providing aggregated child earnings and task completion data to parents.
Cyclical state reset logic that resets task availability status at weekly interval boundaries.
Data retention requirement for temporal task data persistence beyond current cycle.
